title: Ukraine Strikes Two Russian Shadow Fleet Tankers at Novorossiysk Port Entrance
summary: Ukrainian forces struck two shadow fleet tankers in waters at the entrance to Russia's Black Sea port of Novorossiysk on May 3, with President Zelenskyy stating the tankers had actively transported oil and would do so no longer.
contents:
## Ukraine Targets Russian Shadow Fleet at Novorossiysk

### Strike Details
Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on May 3 that Ukrainian forces struck two shadow fleet tankers in waters at the entrance to the Russian Black Sea port of Novorossiysk. "These tankers had actively transported oil. Now they won't," Zelenskyy stated on Telegram.

### Broader Campaign Against Oil Infrastructure
The strikes formed part of a larger wave of Ukrainian attacks targeting Russian oil export infrastructure on May 3. Ukrainian drones also hit Russia's Primorsk port on the Baltic Sea, one of Russia's largest oil export gateways with capacity to handle one million barrels per day.

### Additional Naval Targets
Zelenskyy reported that Ukrainian forces also destroyed a Karakurt-class missile ship, a patrol boat, and another tanker belonging to Russia's shadow fleet near the Primorsk port area. "Each such result further limits Russia's war potential," he wrote.

### Shadow Fleet Significance
The shadow fleet consists of aging tankers used to circumvent Western sanctions and price caps on Russian energy exports. Ukraine's targeting of these vessels directly threatens Russia's ability to maintain oil revenue streams that fund its military operations.

### Market Impact
Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov warned that global oil prices may rise further if Ukraine continues to hit Russia's oil infrastructure, noting prices already exceeded $120 per barrel.

**Sources:** Reuters, AP, Bloomberg
